user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
帮你把书变成可执行方案库的一套skill：book-to-skill，把书当成工作流里的一个插件，按需加载

book-to-skill相当于一个知识编译器，把松散的文档转化为供Agent推理而非检索的结构化Skill，一次编译，后续按需调用

它先提取书籍文本，再让AI分析提取后的合并文本识别章节结构和思维框架，编译成结构化文件，核心索引随查询加载，章节精华按需加载，单次查询约5K token

用Claude Sonnet 4.5处理一本400页的书大约1美元，远低于每次对话都重读PDF的token消耗

适合需要反复资料查阅的场景，比如说技术书/工具书，读完后需要在工作/学习中使用、查阅公司内部文档、查阅品牌规范/合规规范等等

可以跨Agent兼容，支持增量更新

除了书，内部文档、论文集、RFC规范等等皆可，支持PDF、EPUB、DOCX、TXT、Markdown、HTML、RTF、MOBI等格式

#skill #booktoskill

user.description: I help increase online growth and strengthen digital AI moats. Digital & AI mgmt consultant. Get my free email and Tech Strategy podcast.

fullText:
THE 11 RETAIL FORMATS OF JD’S GROWING OFFLINE EMPIRE

Alibaba has been exiting physical retail. 

Selling off its departments stores and hypermarkets.

But ecommerce giant JD is full steam ahead in physical retail.

It has been building a huge offline footprint across China and now Europe.

Here are their current retail formats:

1: JD Mall and E-Space

Their first mega format. JD Mall is the newer version of the original JD E-Space.

These mega-malls range from 30,000 to 50,000 sqm.

Mostly centered on consumer electronics and home appliances.

2: JD Space (京东天地/ Tiandi)

This is a newer mega, urban lifestyle format.

It blends digital commerce, experiential shopping and lifestyle. About 33,000 sqm.

The first location is scheduled to open in September 2026 in Suqian (Liu Qiangdong’s hometown)

3: JD Appliance City Flagship Stores

These stores are one tier down from the mega-malls.

They act as regional anchors for household consumer tech and white goods.

They typically cover 10,000 sqm.

JD is rolling them out aggressively across major regional cities.

4: JD Auto Service

The automotive network has grown rapidly. It now numbers over 4,000 stores.

They offer vehicle maintenance, parts, and integrated O2O (online-to-offline) services.

5: JD Health and Pharmacy

The pharmacy network is massive.

It combines self-operated flagship pharmacies with a partner store network that connects over 150,000 locations nationwide.

6: JD Home

These are specialized experiential stores inside larger malls, focusing on home decor, smart devices, and lifestyle products.

7: Fresh Food and Grocery

7Fresh is JD's premium offline supermarket chain.

It features fresh groceries, dine-in food courts, and rapid 3-kilometer delivery fulfillment.

The network has expanded past the 400-store mark with the 7Fresh Kitchen sub-brand.

8: Ochama and Joybuy

These are JD’s evolving retail brands in the UK and Europe.

9: JD Home Appliance Shops

This asset-lite, franchise network includes +19,000 stores.

It is for China's lower-tier cities and county-level markets.

Over 130 of these locations have been upgraded to regional flagship status.

10: JD Electronics Stores

This network has +3,000 franchise locations. They specialize in mobile phones, laptops, and digital accessories.

11: JD Convenience Stores

The JD Bianli network transforms traditional mom-and-pop shops into more modernized convenience stores.

JD provides independent owners with digital SaaS management systems, smart inventory tracking, and direct supply chain access.

fullText:
New release from @PacktDataML at https://t.co/WyEqNc93yl

"A Practical Guide to Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)"

𝗔𝗺𝗮𝘇𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝘂𝗺𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘆:

RLHF is a powerful approach to AI alignment and human-centered machine learning. By combining reinforcement learning algorithms with human feedback signals, RLHF has become a key method for improving the safety, reliability, and alignment of large language models (LLMs).

This book begins with the foundations of reinforcement learning and policy optimization, including algorithms such as proximal policy optimization (PPO), and explains how reward models and human preference learning help fine-tune AI systems and generative AI models.

fullText:

———
Amazon Summary:

"This book shows you step-by-step and example-by-example how to build sites and applications that take advantage of large language models (LLMs) like GPT, Claude, and Llama. Written especially for web developers comfortable with React or Next.js, this book introduces the tools and techniques you need to add sophisticated AI features like Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), document summarization, chatbots, and more to your web-based projects.

"It guides you through AI development using only JavaScript and other common web dev skills–no Python or Machine Learning experience required. You’ll learn by working with full-scale AI projects that solve actual business problems. You’ll soon be delivering user-friendly, efficient interfaces that make the absolute best use of AI tech."

🔶Hyperparameter Optimization
🔷Ensembling & Stacking Solutions
🔶Modeling Image Classification & Segmentation

My Review (on Amazon):
This 700-page masterpiece of writing covers everything you need—start to finish—to be a competitive coder, specifically for Kaggle data science competitions. The book covers the mechanics of the competitions (platform, resources, rankings, leaderboards), then the infrastructure (notebooks, GitHub, data sets, frameworks, discussion forums), and then nearly 500 pages devoted to "Elevating Your Game" (in-depth coverage of modeling techniques, evaluation metrics, validation strategies, hyperparameter optimization, ensembles, stacking, and various categories of competitions: tabular data, computer vision, NLP, Gen AI, simulations). The book concludes with a valuable section on building your Kaggle portfolio for career advancement and new opportunities. This is an outstanding data science / AI / Machine Learning training resource for anyone, even if you are not into the competitions, though especially if you are a dedicated Kaggler.

user.name: Kirk Borne
user.description: Advisor to startups. Freelancer. @LeadershipData founder. Global Speaker. Top #B2B influencer & social promoter #DataScience #AI #ML. PhD Astrophysics @Caltech

fullText:
Another great book by @AltoValentina, from @PacktDataML at https://t.co/yPRfXTozGF

"AI Agents in Practice — Design, Implement, and Scale Autonomous #AI Systems for Production"

Book Description (from Amazon):

As AI agents evolve to take on complex tasks and operate autonomously, you need to learn how to build these next-generation systems. Author Valentina Alto brings practical, industry-grounded expertise in AI Agents in Practice to help you go beyond simple chatbots and create AI agents that plan, reason, collaborate, and solve real-world problems using large language models (LLMs) and the latest open-source frameworks.

In this book, you'll get a comparative tour of leading AI agent frameworks such as LangChain and LangGraph, covering each tool's strengths, ideal use cases, and how to apply them in real-world projects. Through step-by-step examples, you’ll learn how to construct single-agent and multi-agent architectures using proven design patterns to orchestrate AI agents working together. Case studies across industries will show you how AI agents drive value in real-world scenarios, while guidance on responsible AI will help you implement ethical guardrails from day one. The chapters also set the stage with a brief history of AI agents, from early rule-based systems to today's LLM-driven autonomous agents, so you understand how we got here and where the field is headed.

By the end of this book, you'll have the practical skills, design insights, and ethical foresight to build and deploy AI agents that truly make an impact.

fullText:
一套小红书图文创作Skill：XHS Visual Director，核心解决文字与配图两张皮的问题，使你的内容表达力度直接提升3-5倍

文案工具很多，能把内容长什么样儿想清楚、说清楚的工具很少，搞内容创作的可以看看

给定主题，它会先判断内容任务，再选择适合的视觉风格，然后输出完整的图文方案

首先它会基于内容判断，分析选题属于什么类型，比如技术观点、商业趋势、工具教程等等，推荐风格并给出理由，避免通用模板

再给出6-8页结构，每页只承担一个传播任务

逐页视觉规划都遵循统一的配色/字体/构图/元素规范，确保6-8页是一个整体且保持层次递进

最终给出每页配图提示词、发布文案以及自检清单

相当于给定一个主题，它输出一套完整的视觉方案：从风格判断到逐页规划到生图提示词到发布文案，拿到手就能直接生图发文

user.name: Jeffrey Towson 陶迅

fullText:
WHAT I LEARNED FROM MY VISIT TO CATL HEADQUARTERS

I recently went to CATL HQ in Ningde, Fujian. 

It was a super interesting visit. Which I have written up in 5 articles.

Here are quick 3 take-aways.

1: CATL Has Global Market Leadership AND Growth 

That’s unusual. 

CATL dominates the global battery market with a 39% share in electric vehicle batteries and a 30% share in energy storage systems. 

But it also has high growth in both business lines. 

It’s unusual to have both global dominance and high growth. 

2: CATL Has a 4-Pronged Approach to R&D

The biz structures its research and innovation around four pillars:

a) Material and electrochemistry improvements. 

This is important but science happens slower.

b) Battery system structure optimization. 

This is important and faster (it’s engineering).

c) Intelligent manufacturing. 

This is a lot of reason they are cheaper. Process engineering is a big factor.

d) Business model innovations. 

This includes their move in services, such as battery swapping.
 
3: They Are Currently Commercializing Sodium Ion Batteries 

CATL is moving sodium ion batteries from the laboratory to mass production. Via its Naxtra platform. 

These batteries offer a 20% cost reduction, greater safety, and better low temperature performance compared to lithium ion. 

And sodium is way more available than lithium. Important.

Read here for more on my visit.

https://t.co/mCJxXxZiRd

user.name: Richard Socher
user.description:
Building self-improving superintelligence
CEO @recursive_si and @youdotcom
MP @aixventuresHQ
Ex: Stanford Adj Prof, Chief Scientist at Salesforce, CEO MetaMind

fullText:
Yes, education needs to change because of AI.
But just because AI can now do something better doesn't mean people shouldn't learn that skill. Not every skill we learn has to be immediately useful in a job.

A lot of the training in school and university is around how to be a functional member of that society, how to learn anything (meta-learning), how to memorize anything, how to think and debate on the fly, how to communicate intentions well.

Grades are a signal that can be useful in hiring (there are many others). Not only to know that a student understood that particular skill but also how they compare to their peers, if they can deliver under pressure, do they have the will to succeed.

By taking many subjects students may get lucky and find something they're truly interested in.

Sure, AI knows any fact and has lots of skills that may take you a long time to learn but you'll not be a very interesting conversation partner if you have to constantly get the next most interesting fact or a clever follow-up or counter from an LLM. You'll be easily fooled if you can't do basic math in your head. 

Ultimately, a lot of education may feel like a gym for the mind. Going to the gym isn't useful for humanity and mostly doesn't earn you more money. But it's good for you. It's good to use your muscles and your brain to not waste away. 

What we do need is teaching more agency, creativity and how to clearly communicate intentions and rewards to AI.

fullText:
I have been tracking my own follower count on X for over 11 years, ever since I had 30,000 followers. For the past several years, I have specifically recorded the specific dates when that number crossed past the next 1000, all the way up to 485,000 on March 29 of this year. That was  11 weeks (77 days) ago. My current number (according to my X profile page) is ~485,300 — a mere increase of about 4 per day. X’s own numbers on my Premium Account Analytics page is shown in the chart below — not one day had a net decrease. The average increase per day is around 40-50 (or more) — I can’t tell a specific number from this eye chart. But it is certainly 10X what my profile page says — a real increase of 3000-4000 followers since March, not 300.

I am now hearing from people saying that they “followed” my account but the follow did not register for them. My DAILY X-generated ANALYTICS show it, but my follower count and my new followers don’t see it. 

Other accounts here have been reporting similar problems with their follower counts.

If someone knows a hack to fix this, please post it here, for myself and others. Thanks.

fullText:
给Agent的外挂大脑，解决Agent上线后运维与自主优化的一个工具：Adaline 2.0

这是一个面向生产环境的Agent运维与自动优化平台，核心解决Agent上线后的行为监控、问题暴露、自动生成测试与数据、以及自动迭代的问题

当Agent大规模进入生产环境时，这个需求就比较精准

首先链路追踪，Agent每一步推理/工具调用全量记录，并自动归类重复的行为模式，生成行为地图可视化呈现

然后从真实trace中自动生成测试用例，并生成缺失场景的合成数据，自动生成多个候选Agent并自动测试

等于是给Agent外挂一个自我改进层，让Agent运维变成了一个由数据驱动的自动化工程

#agent运维 #Adaline  #AgentOps
